topics.name: Arrays
topics.description:
An array is like a row of boxes.
Each box has a number and holds one item.
You can quickly find an item if you know its number.
It keeps data organized in a straight line.
topics.realWorldApps:
📚 Books on a shelf: Fixed spots, easy to find.
🎵 Music Playlist: Songs playing in order.

topics.name: Stack
topics.description:
A stack is like a pile of plates.
You only touch the top one.
Last In, First Out (LIFO).
topics.realWorldApps:
🥞 Cafeteria Plates: Take from the top.
↩️ Undo Button: Reverses the last action.

topics.name: Queue
topics.description:
A queue is like a line at a shop.
First person in line is served first.
First In, First Out (FIFO).
topics.realWorldApps:
🎫 Ticket Counter: First come, first served.
🖨️ Printer: Prints documents in order.

topics.name: Linkedlist
topics.description:
A chain of nodes connected together.
Each node holds data and points to the next one.
Items are not stored side-by-side in memory.
topics.realWorldApps:
🚂 Train Wagons: Each car connects to the next.
🔗 Scavenger Hunt: Each clue leads to the next location.
topics.timeComplexity:
🐢 Accessing Item: Slow (Must walk the chain, O(N))
⚡ Insert at Start: Instant (Just update one link, O(1))

topics.name: Tree
topics.description:
A tree is like a folder structure.
It has a root folder and many sub-folders.
Used for hierarchies.
topics.realWorldApps:
📂 File Explorer: Folders inside folders.
🧬 Family Tree: Ancestors and descendants.

topics.name: SearchingAlgorithms
topics.description:
Searching is finding a specific item in a collection.
It answers: 'Is it here?' and 'Where is it?'.
Essential for retrieving data.
topics.realWorldApps:
📞 Phonebook: Finding a contact by name.
🛒 E-commerce: Finding a product by name.
topics.algorithm:
Example: Binary Search (Divide & Conquer)

1. Start with a sorted list.
2. Pick the middle item.
3. Match? You found it! Stop.
4. Too big? Discard the right half.
5. Too small? Discard the left half.
6. Repeat until found or empty.
topics.timeComplexity:
⚡ Binary Search: Very Fast (O(log N))
🐢 Linear Search: Slow (O(N))
